text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet town nestled among tall mountains, there was an old oak tree that had seen countless winters. The tree loved all sorts of creatures that would come to visit – squirrels climbing its branches, birds making homes in its branches, and occasionally a tired hiker resting under its shade.

One day, a little mouse named Mittens ventured out from her burrow to explore the great woodland outside. She skipped happily from branch to branch until she reached the oak. As she sat munching on an acorn, the tree hummed a melodious tune.

"Mittens," the oak sighed, "You've been singing those little bird song melodies all morning. Do you hear them anywhere?"

Mittens paused and listened. Her ears twitched – a woodpecker had drummed its approval into a cheerful warble.

The oak offered her a walnut shell filled with seed grains. "You're quite persuasive, Mittens."

That evening, as twilight embraced the mountains
